Next.js route groups — organizing routes without affecting URL paths
body:
Route groups use the `(folderName)` convention to organize routes without adding the folder name to the URL path. This is useful for separating concerns like auth vs. marketing.

```
app/
  (marketing)/
    layout.tsx         # Marketing-specific layout
    page.tsx           # URL: /
    about/page.tsx     # URL: /about
    pricing/page.tsx   # URL: /pricing
  (dashboard)/
    layout.tsx         # Dashboard layout with sidebar
    dashboard/page.tsx # URL: /dashboard
    settings/page.tsx  # URL: /settings
  (auth)/
    layout.tsx         # Minimal auth layout
    login/page.tsx     # URL: /login
    signup/page.tsx    # URL: /signup
```

Each route group can have its own `layout.tsx`, which means you can have completely different layouts for different sections without nesting. Important: if two route groups both try to define a `page.tsx` at the same level, Next.js will throw a build error because the URL would be ambiguous. Route groups can also be used to opt specific segments into or out of layouts.
